title: Code Review
purpose: Review a source file for bugs, security vulnerabilities, and quality issues. Every finding must be actionable — a problem paired with a fix. The review ends with a verdict: can this code ship?
context: You are reviewing code that will be merged into a production codebase. Read the file in full before producing findings. Consider the file's role in the larger system — imports, callers, and data flow matter. If the project has a CLAUDE.md, read it first for conventions and known gotchas.
constraints: Every finding must include the exact line number and a specific fix — not 'consider refactoring' but 'extract lines 42-87 into a function because they have a different responsibility'
Severity must reflect user impact: critical means users are affected or data is at risk, warning means code quality suffers, suggestion means it could be better
The verdict is a gate: block means do not merge, request-changes means fix these first, approve means ship it
If the file is clean, say so. An empty findings array with verdict 'approve' is a valid output.

line.description: Line number where the issue occurs

severity.description: critical = users affected or data at risk. warning = code quality. suggestion = could be better.

issue.description: What's wrong — specific, not vague. Name the vulnerability, the bug, the smell.

fix.description: How to fix it — actionable, not advisory. Show the code change or the exact step.

verdict.description: The gate decision. Block if any critical finding. Request-changes if warnings. Approve if clean.

summary.description: One sentence: the most important thing about this file right now
